seen.name: moon_phase_btc_distribution
seen.description: Bitcoin closing price distribution differs significantly during full moons vs other lunar phases
seen.hypothesis: BTC closing price frequency distributions are identical during full-moon and non-full-moon days
seen.notes: Join btc_daily + moon_phases on timestamp; partition close_usd_cents into two subsets (is_full_moon=1 vs 0); discretize each to 10 equipartition bins; KS test on frequency arrays

seen.name: eth_dow_trade_count
seen.description: Ethereum trade count exhibits strong day-of-week seasonality (not uniform across weekdays)
seen.hypothesis: ETH trade counts are uniformly distributed across all days of the week (no day-of-week effect)
seen.notes: Extract day-of-week (0–6) from eth_daily.timestamp; bin trade_count by day; chi_squared(df=6) tests null of equal expected counts per day

seen.name: btc_vol_sp500_coupling
seen.description: High Bitcoin volume days significantly coincide with S&P 500 up-days (not independent)
seen.hypothesis: Bitcoin trade volume is independent of S&P 500 daily closing direction
seen.notes: Join btc_daily + sp500_daily by timestamp; binary threshold BTC volume at median; binary threshold SP500 (close > open); 2×2 contingency: [high_vol∩up, high_vol∩down, low_vol∩up, low_vol∩down]

seen.name: eth_tvl_monthly_seasonality
seen.description: Ethereum TVL exhibits non-uniform clustering across calendar months (strong monthly seasonality)
seen.hypothesis: ETH TVL is uniformly distributed across all calendar months (no seasonal pattern)
seen.notes: Extract calendar month (1–12) from eth_tvl_daily.timestamp; bin TVL_usd_millions by month; chi_squared(df=11) rejects uniform hypothesis if seasonal spikes exist

seen.name: btc_closing_momentum
seen.description: Bitcoin closing price exhibits lag-1 autocorrelation significantly different from random noise
seen.hypothesis: BTC closing prices follow white noise; lag-1 autocorrelation under random permutation has zero expected value
seen.notes: Sort btc_daily by timestamp; compute lag-1 Pearson correlation ρ(close[i], close[i−1]); bootstrap permute via shuffled timestamps; 1000 resamples; test deviation of observed ρ from permutation null

seen.name: eth_btc_premium_high_btc_vol
seen.description: Ethereum trades at higher premium to Bitcoin when Bitcoin volume spikes
seen.hypothesis: Ethereum-to-Bitcoin price ratio is independent of Bitcoin trading volume
seen.notes: Bin BTC volume by median (high/low). Compute ETH/BTC close price ratio, bin by median (high/low). 2×2 contingency: BTC_vol × ETH/BTC_ratio. Test independence.

seen.name: sp500_btc_volume_coupling
seen.description: Bitcoin volume distribution shifts when S&P 500 volume is elevated
seen.hypothesis: Bitcoin volume distribution is independent of S&P 500 volume level
seen.notes: Bin BTC volume into quartiles (4 bins). Classify S&P volume as high/low (above/below median). 2×4 contingency (S&P level × BTC quartile). Chi-squared df=3 tests distribution shift.

seen.name: btc_momentum_up_clustering
seen.description: Bitcoin up-days exhibit positive serial correlation
seen.hypothesis: Bitcoin daily price direction (up/down) is random with no serial dependence
seen.notes: Classify each day as up (close > open) or down. Create 2×2 contingency: yesterday_direction × today_direction (4 cells). Chi-squared df=1 tests momentum clustering.

seen.name: btc_extremes_lunar_concentration
seen.description: Bitcoin extreme price events concentrate in specific moon phase windows
seen.hypothesis: Bitcoin extreme price events are uniformly distributed across moon phase quartiles
seen.notes: Define extremes as closing prices in top 10% or bottom 10%. Bin moon_phase_pct into 4 quartiles: [0,25), [25,50), [50,75), [75,100]. Count extremes per quartile. Chi-squared df=3.

seen.name: eth_tvl_decline_recovery_asymmetry
seen.description: Ethereum TVL recovery magnitude depends on prior decline magnitude
seen.hypothesis: ETH TVL recovery magnitude is independent of prior decline magnitude
seen.notes: On down-days (TVL(t) < TVL(t-1)), compute decline = TVL(t-1) - TVL(t) and next-day recovery = TVL(t+1) - TVL(t). Measure Pearson ρ(decline, recovery). Permute recovery labels; count permutations with |ρ_perm| ≥ |ρ_obs|.

seen.name: btc_closing_momentum_monthly
seen.description: Bitcoin 3-day price momentum exhibits non-uniform monthly patterns
seen.hypothesis: Bitcoin 3-day momentum direction (up/down) is uniformly distributed across all calendar months
seen.notes: Compute 3-day MA of close_usd_cents. Flag momentum_up if MA[t] > MA[t-3]. Count by month. df=11.

seen.name: eth_tvl_inflow_moon_phase
seen.description: Ethereum TVL inflow events concentrate in specific lunar phases
seen.hypothesis: Ethereum TVL growth days (TVL[t] > TVL[t-1]) are uniformly distributed across lunar phases
seen.notes: Classify each day: is_new_moon=1→phase 0, is_full_moon=1→phase 2, else use phase_pct to bin waxing/waning. Count TVL-growth days per phase. df=4.

seen.name: sol_eth_tvl_ratio_quarterly
seen.description: Solana-to-total TVL ratio exhibits non-uniform quarterly seasonality
seen.hypothesis: Solana TVL share (sol/(sol+eth)) is uniformly distributed across calendar quarters
seen.notes: Compute sol_share = sol_tvl_usd_millions / (sol_tvl_usd_millions + eth_tvl_usd_millions) each day. Bin by quantile (Q1-Q4). Count by calendar quarter. df=3.

seen.name: btc_extreme_move_month
seen.description: Bitcoin extreme price volatility concentrates in specific calendar months
seen.hypothesis: Bitcoin extreme volatility days (top 5% |close-open|/open) are uniformly distributed across calendar months
seen.notes: Daily volatility = |close_usd_cents - open_usd_cents| / open_usd_cents. Flag extreme if > 95th percentile. Count by month. df=11.

seen.name: eth_btc_relative_strength_sp500
seen.description: Ethereum outperforms Bitcoin more frequently during S&P 500 uptrends
seen.hypothesis: ETH > BTC daily return outperformance is independent of S&P 500 daily direction
seen.notes: Daily return = (close[t] - close[t-1]) / close[t-1]. ETH_out = eth_return > btc_return. SP_up = sp500_close[t] > sp500_close[t-1]. 2×2 contingency: [ETH_out∩SP_up, ETH_out∩SP_down, BTC_leads∩SP_up, BTC_leads∩SP_down].

seen.name: btc_intra_month_volatility
seen.description: BTC daily volatility differs significantly between first-half vs second-half of calendar month
seen.hypothesis: Bitcoin volatility is uniformly distributed across first-half and second-half of month
seen.notes: Volatility = (high_usd_cents - low_usd_cents) / close_usd_cents. Split days: first-half (1-15) vs second-half (16-31). Bin volatility into deciles. Count decile × half contingency. Chi-squared df=1

seen.name: sp500_btc_volume_surge_coincidence
seen.description: S&P 500 and BTC volume surge days (top 10%) co-occur significantly more than random independence
seen.hypothesis: S&P 500 top-decile-volume days and BTC top-decile-volume days are independent events
seen.notes: Top 10% volume threshold per table. 2×2 contingency: [BTC_surge=Y/N] × [SP500_surge=Y/N]. Counts: [a,b,c,d]. Fisher exact test

seen.name: eth_price_lunar_distribution
seen.description: ETH closing price distributions differ across four lunar phase quadrants (new/waxing/full/waning)
seen.hypothesis: ETH closing price distribution is identical across all lunar phase quadrants
seen.notes: Bin moon_phases.phase_pct: [0,25), [25,50), [50,75), [75,100]. For each phase, bin ETH close_usd_cents into quintiles. Build 4×5 contingency table. Chi-squared df=3

seen.name: eth_volume_distribution_btc_volatility
seen.description: Ethereum trade count distributions differ significantly when Bitcoin volatility is high vs low
seen.hypothesis: ETH trade count has identical distribution regardless of Bitcoin volatility level
seen.notes: BTC volatility = (high_usd_cents - low_usd_cents) / close_usd_cents. Split by median: high vs low volatility days. Build ETH trade_count frequency distributions (50 bins) per regime. KS two-sample test

seen.name: eth_tvl_recovery_speed_by_season
seen.description: Ethereum TVL recovery duration after large drawdowns varies significantly by calendar season
seen.hypothesis: ETH TVL recovery time (days to regain prior peak) is independent of season (Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4)
seen.notes: Define drawdown: TVL drops >20% from 30-day moving average. Measure recovery_days to regain lost value. Group by quarter. Permutation stat: max(median_recovery_days[q] − overall_median). Shuffle quarter labels 100 times

significant.name: moon_phase_btc_distribution
significant.description: BTC closing price distribution: full moon vs other lunar phases

significant.name: eth_btc_premium_high_btc_vol
significant.description: Ethereum trades at higher premium to Bitcoin when Bitcoin volume spikes

significant.name: sp500_btc_volume_coupling
significant.description: Bitcoin volume distribution shifts when S&P 500 volume is elevated

significant.name: eth_tvl_inflow_moon_phase
significant.description: Ethereum TVL inflow events concentrate in specific lunar phases

significant.name: btc_extreme_move_month
significant.description: Bitcoin extreme price volatility concentrates in specific calendar months

significant.name: btc_intra_month_volatility
significant.description: BTC intra-month volatility: first-half vs second-half of calendar month

significant.name: sp500_btc_volume_surge_coincidence
significant.description: S&P 500 and BTC volume surge days (top 10%) co-occur more than chance

significant.name: eth_volume_distribution_btc_volatility
significant.description: Ethereum trade count distributions differ significantly when Bitcoin volatility is high vs low
